title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, 39, was arraigned on involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide charges for a crash killing a 5-year-old boy.
Prosecutors allege Charles drove recklessly, dropping the child off unsafely and neglecting a bus safety inspection.
Months after the fatal crash, Charles was involved in a separate hit-and-run, leading to additional charges.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet pitched six strong innings, allowing three hits and walking two, helping the Red Sox shut out the Reds 3-0.
Marcelo Mayer's leadoff double in the seventh inning set up the decisive play, bringing him home for the game's first run.
Roman Anthony went 3-for-4, showing excellent plate discipline, and was key in maintaining Boston's offensive pressure.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, missing since October 2025 from Heywood Hospital, was found in Gardner's Crystal Lake after five months.
Mercury's body was located using drones and retrieved by the State Police Dive Team, Worcester DA's office confirmed.
The cause of Mercury's death is under investigation by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33 from Peabody, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after recovering from a femur tumor surgery.
She was initially advised against running but after a decade of recovery, she is cleared for all physical activities.
Sydney is raising funds for Mass General Hospital, which played a crucial role in her recovery and rehabilitation.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Dr. Jennifer Leah Goetz, 39, from Cambridge, is running her fifth Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Goetz, trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry at MGH, runs to honor young cancer patients and raise funds.
Running began as a therapy for Goetz's depression, becoming a pivotal part of her life and community involvement.

title: Brothers unite for Boston Marathon run honoring family, cancer losses
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin and Conor Nally from Quincy are running the 130th Boston Marathon in memory of family members lost to cancer.
Collin found solace in running after his father and fiancé's mother passed away from cancer in 2023.
This Boston Marathon marks the second time Collin is participating, and the first time for his brother Conor.

title: Mass. has one of the best minor league ballparks in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Polar Park in Worcester ranked No. 6 among USA Today's best minor league ballparks.
Polar Park has sold over 500,000 tickets annually since its 2021 opening.
Worcester Red Sox mascot, Smiley Ball, was ranked No. 3 among minor league mascots.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 26, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 7th Street Burger opens on Newbury Street, with a South Boston location launching April 26.
Far Out Ice Cream expands with a new location at Cleveland Circle opening April 19.
Gemma Kitchen & Bar set to open in Norwood's old firehouse by late April.

title: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted in deadly Woburn crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for motor vehicle homicide in 2023 crash in Woburn that killed Angelo Schettino.
Prosecutors allege Quigley was drunk, negligent; crash involved his State Police cruiser hitting Schettino's van.
Quigley's past delayed unrelated murder retrial; Suffolk DA handling case as he worked in Middlesex County.

title: Framingham’s iconic ‘castle’ hotel has entered a new era. Take a look inside.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The former Sheraton Framingham Hotel is now The Framingham Hotel Collection after multi-year renovations.
The tri-brand hotel includes Renaissance, Fairfield Inn & Suites, and TownPlace Suites with 371 rooms total.
Three new dining options include Malone's Irish Pub & Social, Provisions Hearth & Kitchen, and Tara Cafe.
